generated_assessment.reasoning: High neuroticism and anxiety (78th pct) combined with low extraversion (17th pct) and anger/hostility (86th pct) fuel skepticism toward expanded military spending, which is perceived as a high-stakes, anxiety-provoking policy that may escalate conflict rather than ensure security. Modest agreeableness (63rd pct) and tender-mindedness (56th pct) reinforce a humanitarian perspective that prioritizes non-military solutions, while moderately low openness (41st pct) and low conscientiousness (34th pct) do not override the core emotional resistance to increased defense spending.
